title: BABIES FIRST! - 211info
text: HOOD RIVER COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A public health nurse (PHN) home visiting program that partners with families to improve pregnancy, family and child health outcomes. Provides health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 1109 June Street Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Perinatal people, infants and children 0-5, and parent or primary caregivers with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, drug-exposed infants. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 386-1115 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 386-1115 Main phone Main phone (541) 386-1115 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or visit website FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Varies by site LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Hood River County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Case/Care Management * Families with Infants/Toddlers Home Based Parenting Education Nutrition Education * Families with Infants/Toddlers Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BRIDGES TO HEALTH PATHWAYS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HOOD RIVER COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT
